column1: # Use For: Teaching when to use exponential models
# Scenario: Nutrient intake and deficiency risk
#   - Low doses: Risk decreases rapidly with each unit
# The relationship follows: RR = exp(β₀ + β₁ × (1 - e^(-α×dose)))
# Model: GLS (Quadratic) - Will NOT fit well!
#   - β₂ will be significant but wrong direction
#   - Model may predict decreasing then increasing (incorrect)
#   - Risk decreases rapidly at low doses
#   1. NOT all dose-response relationships are polynomial!
#   4. Always plot your data before choosing model
#   5. Compare AIC/BIC between models to choose best fit
